strengths: Strong SQL and query optimization experience
Experience with ETL and data validation pipelines
Demonstrated quantified impact on data accuracy and efficiency
weaknesses: No explicit Microsoft SQL Server / SSRS mention
Limited tenure in public sector or legal/court systems
No explicit Office of Court Administration or NYPD integration experience
No stated experience with sensitive criminal history protocols
Certifications in MS SQL/SSRS not listed
improvement_bullets: Optimized relational database queries (using Microsoft SQL Server and CTEs/window functions) to clean and reconcile insurance testing data, improving data accuracy by 30%.
Developed robust SQL reporting protocols and data audit workflows to ensure data integrity for cross-functional departments, mirroring professional data management standards.
Collaborated with IT teams to improve in-house application efficiency, documenting workflows to ensure data consistency across multiple source systems.
Demonstrated a high level of discretion and diplomacy when managing confidential data, ensuring compliance with strict organizational data handling and security protocols.
Leveraged Power BI to build automated reporting suites, effectively managing competing priorities to deliver high-quality data insights under strict deadlines.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst opportunity with the District Attorney Kings County. I am interested in this opportunity because of the Office’s commitment to ensuring fairness and transparency through data-driven initiatives. What interests me about working for the District Attorney Kings County is its mission to keep Brooklyn safe while building community trust, which inspires me because I am passionate about using technical expertise to support social justice. I believe my expertise in SQL data auditing and my proficiency in Power BI and data reconciliation would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on data integrity and process improvement. In fact, while at Haven Technologies, I was tasked with cleaning complex insurance data that spanned multiple sources. I utilized Microsoft SQL Server to write optimized queries and window functions to identify and resolve inconsistencies, which resulted in a 30% improvement in data accuracy. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst role at the District Attorney Kings County would allow me to bring my SQL auditing skills to the Case Management Services Unit to ensure the accuracy of court proceeding data. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my project prioritization skills. During this time, I managed multiple competing data requests while automating reporting workflows. I successfully reduced data processing time by 40% and improved KPI visibility for executive leadership, a result that directly translates to the high-stakes environment of the DA’s office. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my analytical and problem-solving skills in working on data integrity reports at the District Attorney Kings County. I know that my SQL expertise and commitment to data accuracy align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
